title: Bridge Essay: Touching and being touched: where knowing and feeling meet.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Liberation/Enlightenment** (Phase 15) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Liberation/Enlightenment to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Touching and being touched: where knowing and feeling meet.** — Front Psychol. PMID: 37533722. DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2023.1097402. Philosophers maintain that touch confers asense of reality or grounding to perceptual experience. In touching oneself, one is simultaneously both subject and object of touch, atemplate for experiencing oneself as subject and object of intentions, feelings, and motivations, or intersubjectivity. Here, Iexplore aform of self-touch carefully documented by Winnicott in observing how the infant engages the transitional object. Icompare the processes of self-loss in transitional states, including
